Either extend retention to exceed the realistic max-offline (design suggests 7d), OR have reconcile treat 'missing-on-server for a path the local shadow HAS a record for' as 'server deleted since we synced' -> stash-and-honor-delete rather than blind re-create. Shadow presence + server-absence is itself the delete signal; don't need the tombstone if shadow is consulted.

attack_scenarios.scenario: Server serves stale bytes during the FS-materialize commit gap. Even with FS-materialize-on-push, there is a window: CAS commits PG.body=B at T; the os.replace(FS<-B) happens at T+epsilon. A GET /api/sync/note arriving in (T, T+epsilon) reads the OLD FS file (still A) because get_note is FS-preferred (sync_routes_p1.py:~1075 reads fs_resolved bytes, falls back to PG only if no FS file). So a subscriber fetching in that window materializes A with sha(A)==its own integrity check passing, records shadow=sha(A)... but the design says shadow should record payload.sha256 which the SERVER computed -- if the server's NotePayload.sha256 came from PG (=B) while body_raw came from FS (=A), the integrity check (materializer.rs:556 compares written-sha vs payload.sha256) MISMATCHES -> IntegrityFailed -> file kept, no shadow record -> next reconcile re-pulls. Self-heals, but the FS-vs-PG read inconsistency in get_note (body from FS, metadata from PG) is a latent split-brain the design does not address: it assumes FS==PG post-push, but the commit gap and the N-writer race (scenario 1) both violate that.
attack_scenarios.gap: get_note reads body from FS but sha256/metadata can come from PG (_fetch_note_from_vault_notes) -> during the push commit gap (PG=B, FS=A) it can serve body=A with sha=B, failing the daemon integrity check. The FS-materialize must be inside the SAME txn boundary as the CAS commit (or get_note must serve body and sha from the SAME source atomically). As written, FS and PG are read from independently and can disagree.

attack_scenarios.scenario: Fenced/dead watcher with pending==0 stays invisible -- design FIX is a counter, not a gate. Confirmed is_stalled (sync_health.rs:126) = pending>0 && stale. The design proposes a watcher heartbeat + watcher_fenced atomic and 'watchdog fires on fenced OR heartbeat-stale REGARDLESS of pending'. That is the correct fix IF the watchdog actually reads watcher_fenced/heartbeat. But the design only describes ADDING the heartbeat into SyncHealth; it does not specify the watchdog's recovery ACTION for a fenced watcher (the storm-fence at file_watcher.rs:901 HALTS the watcher and 'will NOT auto-resume -- restart the daemon'). So even with detection, the documented recovery is 'restart the daemon' -- which no component does automatically. A fenced watcher is DETECTED but not RECOVERED; local edits silently stop propagating UP until a human restarts. On Linux inotify ENOSPC at startup (file_watcher.rs:681-690) the watch never establishes and pending stays 0 forever.
attack_scenarios.gap: Detecting a fenced/dead watcher (heartbeat/fenced atomic) is necessary but the design specifies no automatic RECOVERY -- the storm-fence explicitly requires a manual daemon restart, and nothing auto-restarts. A detected-but-unrecovered watcher means local edits silently never push. Must wire the watchdog to actually re-establish the watch (or restart the watcher task / the process) on fenced-or-stale, and surface a LOUD tray banner + push a health alert, not just bump a counter.

attack_scenarios.scenario: Dedup-drops-real-edit (FM1) fix depends on a shadow record that the failed push never creates -- correct, but the revert-detection has its own hole. Design: gate dedup on the ShadowStore last-server-accepted hash, not enqueued_hashes, so A->(push dropped 400)->revert-to-A re-enqueues. Trace: enqueue shaA; push 400 skip+ack (push_client.rs:519, returns Skipped) -> NO shadow record (shadow only records on accepted materialize/push). User reverts file to shaA content. New fix: dedup checks shadow.get(path)==shaA? shadow has NO entry (push was 400'd) -> not deduped -> re-enqueues. GOOD. BUT the 400-skip path (push_client.rs:519 ServerRejected) ALSO acks/consumes the journal entry as Skipped -- meaning the ORIGINAL shaA edit was already dropped and never retried. The revert re-enqueues, but if the path is genuinely scope-excluded (the actual reason for a 400 per the comment 'path excluded by V9 baseline scope filter'), the re-enqueue will 400 AGAIN -> infinite re-enqueue/400 loop on every save of a scope-excluded file the user keeps editing. The design's dedup-on-shadow fix trades a silent-drop for a potential hot-loop on scope-excluded paths.
attack_scenarios.gap: Gating dedup on shadow (not enqueued_hashes) correctly fixes the silent-drop, but for genuinely scope-EXCLUDED paths (the real cause of HTTP 400) it creates a re-enqueue/400 churn loop on every edit. Distinguish 'transient drop' (re-enqueue) from 'permanent scope-exclusion 400' (suppress AND surface to the user that this path is not syncing) -- a permanent-reject path should record a 'do-not-retry' marker, not silently 400-loop.

attack_scenarios.scenario: change_seq re-fan amplification after the revert is fixed only if BOTH the reconciler guard AND push-FS-materialize land; partial deploy is worse. The body-gated change_seq trigger (migration 2026_05_31_002 region) re-bumps seq on any body change incl. the reconciler's revert, re-fanning A to all subscribers. The design's PRIMARY fix is stopping the reconciler revert. But the rollout deploys the reconciler guard and the push-FS-materialize in Phase 1 'in that order, reconciler fix FIRST'. If the reconciler guard ships but push-FS-materialize does NOT (e.g. push container lacks rw vault mount -- the design's own Phase-0b risk), then: push still writes PG-only, FS stays stale-A, reconciler now SKIPS the revert (guard: PG is newer push) -> PG=B, FS=A permanently. get_note is FS-preferred -> serves A. Subscribers fetch A, integrity-check against payload.sha256: if sha256 comes from PG(B) -> mismatch -> IntegrityFailed loop; if from FS(A) -> they materialize A and shadow=A, diverging from PG=B forever. The 'reconciler-fix-first' ordering without push-FS-materialize creates a PERMANENT FS/PG split-brain that the old unconditional revert actually 'healed' (badly, by reverting). Removing the revert without fixing the FS write can be a net regression for read-consistency.
attack_scenarios.gap: Phase-1 'reconciler guard first, FS-materialize maybe later (gated on rw mount)' creates a permanent PG/FS split-brain (PG=new, FS=old, get_note serves old) that the old buggy revert at least kept eventually-consistent. The two server fixes are NOT independently deployable. Either deploy them atomically, or make get_note PG-body-preferred (serve vault_notes.body, ignore the stale FS) the SAME release as the reconciler guard, so removing the revert does not strand readers on stale FS bytes. Verify the push container rw vault mount in Phase-0b is a HARD gate, not a 'recommend'.

critiques.verdict: REFUTED -- the design does NOT end the bug for good as written. It correctly diagnoses all three silent-clobber engines (verified at materializer.rs:487-519, verify_repair.rs:241-252, reconciler.py:434-438), correctly identifies the live-SSE bypass (sse.rs:271-275 blindly calls write()), correctly confirms production runs ServerWins (lib.rs:510-513 uses Default=ServerWins at materializer.rs:194 with no override), and the ALWAYS-STASH + decide()-unification + NFC + change_seq-ordering direction is sound and matches Obsidian/Syncthing/Dropbox precedent. The daemon-side fixes (R1-R5, stash-on-divergence, per-path lock, dedup-on-shadow, watcher heartbeat) close the SINGLE-HOST, daemon-mediated paths well. BUT the cross-OS/concurrency/crash lens surfaces SIX survives=false gaps that re-admit data loss or silent divergence: (1) the centerpiece FS-materialize-on-push adds an Nth uncoordinated server-side FS writer racing the >=8 confirmed VAULT_ROOT writers (brain/references/depositor/reconciler/meetbot/vault_adapter) with NO shared FS lock -- the CAS row lock is PG-only and the daemon per-path mutex is one host's in-process lock, structurally unable to serialize server-side Python writers; (2) a push CAS-409 loser is NEVER stashed (stash is pull-path-only), so a genuine local edit that loses the race is lost if fanout is dormant; (3) the ~15 EXTERNAL agent writers never go through materializer.write(), so the compare->rename TOCTOU (materializer.rs:469->544) loses an external edit landing in that window with no stash; (4) conflict-copy files are NOT push-excluded (verify_repair HARDCODED_EXCLUDES lacks *.conflict-from-*) and echo_guard records the canonical path not the stash path, so stash copies get pushed up and re-conflict across hosts; (5) on reconnect the stale pre-offline journal drains and 409s edits away BEFORE reconcile can classify-and-stash them; (6) the reconciler-guard-first-without-FS-materialize rollout ordering creates a PERMANENT PG/FS split-brain that the old buggy revert actually masked. Plus a detected-but-unrecovered fenced watcher (the design adds detection but no auto-recovery; the storm-fence still demands a manual restart) and a 24h-tombstone delete-resurrection. The design is APPROXIMATELY 70% complete: it ends the bug for the single-host daemon-mediated case, but the server-side multi-writer FS race (gap 1) is the same class of silent revert it claims to kill, now relocated server-side, and is the hard blocker.
must_fix_before_ship: SERVER-SIDE FS-WRITE SERIALIZATION (the hard blocker): FS-materialize-on-push adds an Nth FS writer to the >=8 confirmed VAULT_ROOT writers (grep: references.py, brain/tools.py, brain/engine.py, brain/digest.py, vault_adapter.py, meetbot/wrap_up.py, reconciler.py). The CAS row lock is PG-only; it does NOT serialize FS writes. Either (a) hold a PG advisory lock pg_advisory_xact_lock(hashtext(storage_key)) across BOTH the PG commit AND the FS os.replace AND make brain/note_writer/references/depositor take the SAME lock around their FS writes, OR (b) adopt the PG-body-preferred read alternative: get_note serves vault_notes.body (the CAS-protected source of truth), stop reading/hashing the FS file for /api/sync/note, and let the reconciler treat PG as authoritative. Without one of these, the live bug is merely relocated from daemon to server with no daemon defense possible.

DEPLOY THE TWO SERVER FIXES ATOMICALLY (or make get_note PG-preferred in the same release): 'reconciler-guard-first, FS-materialize-later (gated on rw mount)' creates a permanent PG/FS split-brain (PG=new, FS=stale, FS-preferred get_note serves stale) that the old unconditional revert masked. Verify the push container rw vault mount (Phase-0b) as a HARD gate, and if it lacks rw, ship get_note PG-body-preferred WITH the reconciler guard so removing the revert does not strand readers on stale FS bytes.

STASH ON PUSH CAS-409: add a stash-the-local-bytes step in push_client on Err(Conflict{expected_hash}) (push_client.rs:511) BEFORE consuming the journal entry. Today the stash floor is pull-path-only; a genuine local edit that loses the CAS race is silently dropped if no subsequent SSE event re-classifies it as R4 (and fanout can be dormant). This is required for the operator ruling 'local edits propagate UP, never silently reverted' to hold on the push side.

EXCLUDE CONFLICT COPIES FROM SYNC + FIX ECHO-GUARD KEYING: add *.conflict-from-*.md to BOTH file_watcher HARDCODED_EXCLUDES and verify_repair HARDCODED_EXCLUDES so conflict copies stay local-only and never push/re-fan/re-conflict across hosts. Separately, echo_guard.record must use the ACTUAL stash path (not payload.path / the canonical) or the watcher will push the stash file up. Without this, the always-stash fix spawns a cross-host conflict-copy storm.

CLOSE THE EXTERNAL-WRITER TOCTOU + MAKE RECONCILE THE RE-VERIFYING SAFETY NET: the per-path dashmap lock only serializes the daemon's own SSE-vs-reconcile calls; the ~15 external agent writers (Obsidian/Edit/scripts) write the vault directly and never take it. Re-read + re-compare the local file UNDER the per-path lock IMMEDIATELY before the rename (materializer.rs ~:544); if it changed since the initial compare (materializer.rs:469), abort-and-restash as fresh divergence. The periodic reconcile backstop MUST re-hash from disk (not trust a stale manifest hash) so it catches any edit that slipped the rename window.

RECONNECT ORDERING: on reconnect, run reconcile-and-stash (or invalidate the stale pre-offline push_journal and re-derive direction from local/shadow/server) BEFORE draining the pre-offline journal. As written, the journal drains first and 409s every server-advanced edit away with no stash, before reconcile can classify them R4-conflict. Couple this with the stash-on-409 fix.

WIRE ACTUAL WATCHER RECOVERY, not just detection: the heartbeat/fenced atomic detects a dead watcher, but the storm-fence (file_watcher.rs:901) HALTS with 'will NOT auto-resume -- restart the daemon' and nothing auto-restarts. The watchdog must re-establish the inotify/FSEvents/ReadDirectoryChangesW watch (or restart the watcher task / process) on fenced-or-stale REGARDLESS of pending==0, AND raise a loud tray banner. Detection without recovery still means local edits silently stop propagating.

SHADOW-WIPE FAST-PATH: on empty/corrupt shadow (sync_shadow.rs:57 loads EMPTY), do NOT let every drift fall to R5-conflict on 28k notes (data-safe but an O(N) conflict-copy + tray-walk_dir avalanche). First pull a full server manifest and seed shadow=server for all paths whose local already==server, so only genuinely-divergent files conflict. Add a hard version gate refusing reconcile/SSE-overwrite until shadow_schema_version>=NFC_MIGRATED so an intermediate daemon build can never silent-overwrite non-ASCII edits.

DELETE-RESURRECTION via shadow, not tombstone TTL: a >24h-offline host re-creates an intentionally-deleted note (missing-on-server -> Push). Fix by consulting shadow: 'shadow HAS a record for this path AND server has no row' = server deleted since we synced -> honor the delete (optionally stash local first), do NOT re-create. This removes the dependence on a 24h tombstone retention that is shorter than realistic offline windows.
